RULING; Argued and the court TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. PARTIES TO SUBMIT RESPECTIVE ORDERS. (302/REQ/JT)BELDON YEE #2647

Mtn To Compel Compliance With Depo Subpoena
Off Calendar Jun-24-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's motion to compel compliance with the deposition subpoena is granted. The Court finds good cause to permit the deposition of counsel for plaintiff as to the circumstances and content of the negotiations and communications with representatives of the defendant, subject to all proper objections, including but not limited to those based on the attorney-client privilege and the work product rule. The Court notes that portions of the correspondence between counsel for plaintiff and defendant involve a limited waiver of the attorney- client privilege and are the proper subject of inquiry. The deposition shall be scheduled at a time mutually convenient to the parties and is to be completed by July 25, 2005. Prevailing party to submit form of order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
